---
title: "MNA assembly & linear solve"
subtitle: "T3 ┬╖ Phase T3.1 ┬╖ `va-core::mna`/`linsolve`"
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Status: code complete.** Dense Modified Nodal Analysis assembly plus a `faer`-backed LU solve
with real singularity detection. `va-core` is staff-maintained shared infrastructure
(`CLAUDE.md` ┬з3), not a student thesis тАФ no T3 owner was found before this mechanics was already
implemented and green.
:::

## Goal

Turn every device's own `StampSink` contributions into one dense linear system, and solve it тАФ
the two pieces every later Newton iteration (`t3-core/02-newton.qmd`) calls in a loop.

## Where it fits

```
 &[&dyn ModelInstance] тФАтФА[mna::assemble]тФАтФАтЦ║ System{residual, jacobian} тФАтФА[linsolve::solve_dense]тФАтФАтЦ║ dx
```

`va-core` depends on `va-abi` and **nothing else** (`CLAUDE.md` ┬з2's load-bearing invariant) тАФ
it never touches `va-ir`/`va-codegen`, so it can be built and validated entirely against
`va-abi`'s own hand-written reference models, independent of whether the frontend/codegen
theses have shipped anything yet.

## The idea

**`System` implements `StampSink` directly** тАФ assembly is just "let every instance load itself
into a zeroed dense buffer, then read the buffer back." There's no intermediate representation
between a device's stamp calls and the matrix; `assemble` is four lines:

```rust
pub fn assemble(instances: &[&dyn ModelInstance], x: &[f64], dim: usize) -> System {
    let mut sys = System::new(dim);
    for inst in instances {
        inst.load(x, &mut sys);
    }
    sys
}
```

**The `Node`/`Branch` distinction lives here, not in the ABI's data shape.** `va_abi::UnknownKind`
(Interface ╬▓) only tells `va-core` which *kind* of row a given global index is; `mna::
classify_unknowns` is what actually walks every instance and builds the per-unknown lookup table
a convergence aid (`gmin` stepping, `t3-core/03-nonlinear-dc.qmd`) needs to know which rows are
safe to shunt. `classify_abstol` is the structurally identical collection for a different
per-unknown property (a discipline's own `abstol`, ┬з the nature-metadata wiring in
`t1-frontend/03-elaboration.qmd`) тАФ same "default, then let any instance override its own
index" shape, reused rather than duplicated.

**Dense, deliberately, for now.** `linsolve::solve_dense` uses `faer`'s partial-pivoted LU тАФ
pure Rust, no BLAS/LAPACK/KLU FFI (`CLAUDE.md` ┬з5's no-native-deps rule, enforced by `deny.toml`).
Dense is the right *starting* choice for a bring-up-scale circuit; the roadmap's own stated
direction is a sparse solve once circuit sizes actually demand it, not a premature optimization.

**Singularity is checked two ways, not one.** A zero pivot in floating point doesn't always
produce a clean `NaN`/`inf` тАФ it can produce a *finite*, wrong answer that merely fails to
reproduce the right-hand side. `solve_dense` checks both: every solved value must be finite,
*and* `тАЦA┬╖x тИТ bтАЦтИЮ` must be small relative to `тАЦbтАЦ` тАФ the residual check catches the near-singular
case partial pivoting alone would otherwise silently pass through as garbage.

## The code

```rust
pub struct System {
    pub residual: Vec<f64>,     // f(x), length dim
    pub jacobian: Vec<f64>,     // df/dx, dense row-major dim*dim
}

pub fn classify_unknowns(instances: &[&dyn ModelInstance], dim: usize) -> Vec<UnknownKind>;
pub fn classify_abstol(instances: &[&dyn ModelInstance], dim: usize, default: f64) -> Vec<f64>;
pub fn assemble(instances: &[&dyn ModelInstance], x: &[f64], dim: usize) -> System;

pub fn solve_dense(a: &[f64], b: &[f64], n: usize) -> Result<Vec<f64>, CoreError>;
```

`System::shunt_gmin` adds a `gmin`-style conductance to ground at every `Node`-kind row only тАФ
"the one place the `Node`/`Branch` distinction actually matters" (its own doc comment): doing
this to a `Branch` row (e.g. a voltage source's own `V(p)-V(n)=value` constraint) would corrupt
the constraint outright, not aid convergence.

## It works

```console
$ cargo test -p va-core mna:: linsolve::
test mna::tests::sink_accumulates_resistor_stamp ... ok
test mna::tests::assemble_sums_parallel_resistors ... ok
test mna::tests::classify_unknowns_tags_vsource_branch_only ... ok
test mna::tests::classify_abstol_lets_one_instance_override_its_own_unknown ... ok
test mna::tests::shunt_gmin_skips_branch_rows ... ok
test linsolve::tests::solves_2x2 ... ok
test linsolve::tests::singular_matrix_is_rejected ... ok
test linsolve::tests::empty_system_is_trivial ... ok
```

`classify_unknowns_tags_vsource_branch_only` is the concrete proof the Node/Branch split works
end to end: a resistor divider with a voltage source correctly reports the source's own branch
current as the *only* `Branch`-kind row among three unknowns тАФ both node rows stay `Node`-kind
even though the source also touches them.

## Limitations

**Dense only.** `linsolve::solve_dense` is `O(n^3)` in circuit size тАФ fine at bring-up scale
(the ladder circuits here have single-digit unknowns), the wrong choice once a real compact
model's internal-node count or a multi-device circuit grows large. A sparse solve is the roadmap's
own stated next step for this crate, not attempted here.

**No pivoting strategy beyond `faer`'s own partial pivoting** тАФ sound for a well-conditioned MNA
system, but nothing here compensates for a genuinely badly-scaled circuit (e.g. mixing pF
capacitances with M╬й resistances) beyond what convergence aids (`t3-core/03-nonlinear-dc.qmd`)
already provide at the Newton level.

## What's next

`t3-core/02-newton.qmd` тАФ the iterative loop that calls `assemble`/`solve_dense` repeatedly to
find a nonlinear circuit's actual operating point.
